name: nitrogen_vent_system_9f8221db
description: Design a simple gaseous nitrogen venting system consisting of a nitrogen gas source node connected to ambient through a single orifice. The nitrogen node contains gaseous nitrogen at elevated pressure and vents to ambient conditions (approximately 1 atm = 101325 Pa) through the orifice connection. The system demonstrates gas flow from high to low pressure through a flow restriction. FIXED: nitrogen node initial pressure (elevated above ambient), ambient node pressure (~1 atm), orifice as the single flow path. TUNABLE: exact initial nitrogen pressure, orifice size/flow coefficient, venting duration.

notes: Nitrogen source should start at elevated pressure above ambient to drive flow
Ambient node represents atmospheric conditions at ~1 bar
Single orifice connection provides the flow restriction
System should show mass flow from nitrogen_source to ambient

checks.description: System exhibits non-zero mass flow through the orifice
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
Ambient node pressure is approximately atmospheric (101325 Pa ± 100 Pa)
Ambient node pressure is approximately atmospheric (101325 Pa ± 100 Pa)
Nitrogen source starts at elevated pressure above ambient
Orifice connection shows mass flow from nitrogen to ambient
